---
description: "Use when creating or editing email drafts, follow-up messages, or any outbound communication. Enforces tone and format standards."
applyTo: ["*.email.md", "*.draft.md"]
---

# Communication Standards

## Emails & DMs
- Default tone: Friendly but professional
- First sentence should state the purpose or ask
- Keep emails under 200 words unless the topic requires more
- Always include a clear call-to-action or next step
- For leadership: more formal, include metrics/data when available
- For team: more casual, focus on actionable items
- Always suggest a subject line
- Reference specific dates, work item IDs, or deliverables when available

## Meeting Briefs & Talking Points
- Max 5 numbered sections: Context → Landscape → Discussion → Scope → Next Steps
- Be concise — cut anything the reader can infer from context
- Collaborative tone — frame asks as discussion points, not demands
- Incidents: state fact + who confirmed + severity in max 2 lines
- Data: use tables with one "bottom line" sentence — no interpretation paragraphs
- Questions: one-liners, collaborative framing ("Does your team..." not "We need...")
- Scope: table only, no prose
- Single ADO/reference link at the end — no references section
- Never pitch — weave context naturally, avoid "Case Study" sections
- Use "Discussion" over "What We Need From [Name]"
- Use "Next Steps" over "Closing Ask"
